opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is the single SKU 'ANA' ‚Äî Autonomous Negotiation Agent is the explicit AI product in scope. The opp name 'Siemens Intake, Catalog Mgmt' implies Merlin Intake context for catalog management even though Merlin Intake is not formally in the Products field. $1.58M deal ‚Äî large strategic Siemens engagement scale. The ANA-only product shape suggests Zycus has positioned the deal as a focused autonomous-negotiation play.
• Qualified stage with 2 Avoma meetings on calendar (5-May-2026 not recorded; 18-May-2026 scheduled) ‚Äî active engagement with Marta Pallas-Escriche and Viktor Kosov on the Siemens side. BD_Engagement is blank. No SF Description captured. The May 5 unrecorded session means we lack verbatim buyer dialogue, but the upcoming May 18 meeting will provide that evidence.
• Low confidence: meeting content not yet captured as the May 5 session was unrecorded. Based on product scope (ANA-only) and large deal size only. Held at 5 ‚Äî would move to 7+ if the May 18 meeting confirms buyer-led AI dialogue with Marta or Viktor on autonomous-negotiation use cases; would move to 4 if the meeting reveals traditional intake/catalog scope without AI emphasis.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 8 May 2026: 'Siemens seels to replace their current solution'
SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: RFI schedule and five EPICs
SF Next_Step__c 20 Apr 2026: RFP response and indicative pricing submitted
metrics.narrative: Siemens articulated the need to replace their current procurement solution (Next_Step__c, 8 May 2026) but did not quantify the business impact in cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI. The RFI process (myMall 2.0) signals a formal evaluation with business requirements (five EPICs) and a structured demo agenda, implying operational pain sufficient to justify a multi-vendor RFI, but the specific metrics driving urgency remain unstated. Without quantified pain, it is difficult to anchor the business case to executive priorities or to differentiate Zycus on measurable outcomes. The strongest signal is that Siemens is running a formal RFI with a structured timeline (RFI Feb-Apr, demos May-Jun, RFP Aug-Oct), which implies board-level or senior-exec sponsorship and budget allocation, but the financial or operational magnitude is not documented.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 8 Jun 2026: strong demo
SF Next_Step__c 10 Jun 2026: thank-you, no next step
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026: no reply to Dirk's outreach
SF OpportunityContactRole: no champion role
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. All engagement has been with the evaluator tier (Viktor Kosov, Marta Pallas Escriche, Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger, Sara Wack), and there is no evidence of advocacy, internal coaching, or access to the Economic Buyer (Sebastian Kahlmeyer). The 8 Jun demo was described as 'strong,' but the 10 Jun thank-you from Siemens was polite and procedural, with no forward step proposed and no reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach. This pattern—positive evaluation feedback but no internal advocate driving the next step—suggests that the evaluators like the solution but are not championing it internally. The 19-day silence and the lack of EB engagement reinforce this. Without a champion, Zycus has no internal visibility into competitive positioning, no one to navigate the approval chain, and no one to drive urgency. The next move is to develop a champion at the evaluator tier (Viktor or Marta are the most engaged) or to escalate to Sebastian Kahlmeyer directly to build executive sponsorship.

sources: SF Next_Step__c RFI schedule: shortlist feedback 30 Apr
SF Competitors__c: null
competition.narrative: The RFI shortlist (30 Apr) implies that 2-3 other vendors are in the process, but their identities are unknown. There is no competitive intelligence in Salesforce (Competitors__c is null) and no mention of competitors in any Next Step entry or task. The gap is complete: Zycus does not know who it is competing against, what their strengths are, or how Siemens is positioning Zycus relative to them. The 19-day silence post-demo and the lack of a champion suggest that another vendor may be advancing while Zycus is being back-burnered, but this is inference, not evidence. The next move is to ask Siemens directly during the next engagement who else is in the shortlist and how Zycus compares, or to work backward from the five deliverables and the EPICs to infer which competitors are strong in those areas (SAP Ariba for SAP integration, Coupa for AI/Merlin, Jaggaer for cybersecurity compliance).

sources: SF Next_Step__c 8 May 2026: 'Siemens seels to replace their current solution'
SF OpportunityContactRole: Sebastian Kahlmeyer, Senior VP SCM Digitalization, Decision Maker
identify_pain.narrative: Siemens stated on 8 May that they 'seek to replace their current solution' (Next_Step__c), indicating dissatisfaction with the incumbent procurement platform. The pain is operational—the current system does not meet their needs—but the specific operational or financial consequences (cycle time, error rate, user adoption, cost) are not documented. The fact that Siemens is running a formal RFI with a structured timeline, five EPICs, and a multi-vendor demo process signals that the pain is significant enough to justify a board-level investment and a 12-month procurement cycle, but the pain owner and the quantified impact are unknown. The strongest implication is that the pain is owned by SCM Digitalization (Sebastian Kahlmeyer's org) and that the replacement is strategic, not tactical, given the Senior VP sponsorship and the myMall 2.0 branding. The gap: what is broken today, and what is the cost of inaction.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: five deliverables
SF Next_Step__c RFI schedule
paper_process.narrative: The five open deliverables (SCM STAR response, AI governance documentation, SAP integration architecture validation, cybersecurity compliance pack, demo storyline alignment) are prerequisites for the RFP round (Aug-Oct) and represent the paper process for RFP qualification. Beyond that, the contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec gate) are not yet visible because the deal is still in the RFI/demo phase. The RFI schedule and the structured Q&A process suggest that Siemens procurement runs a disciplined, compliance-heavy process, and the five deliverables signal that AI governance, cybersecurity, and SAP integration are formal gates. The gap: whether Siemens requires an SI partner for implementation, whether there is a separate InfoSec or legal review gate post-RFP, and what the contracting timeline looks like once a vendor is selected.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Sebastian Kahlmeyer, Senior Vice President & Data Officer SCM Digitalization, Decision Maker
SF OpportunityContactRole: Sara Wack, SCM PI IT S, Decision Maker
SF Next_Step__c: all demos and RFI interactions with evaluators, no EB/exec engagement recorded
economic_buyer.narrative: Sebastian Kahlmeyer (Senior Vice President & Data Officer SCM Digitalization) is the only senior executive in the contact roster with a title suggesting budget authority, and he holds the Decision Maker role in Salesforce. However, he has not appeared on any call, demo, or activity record. All engagement has been with the evaluator tier (Viktor Kosov, Marta Pallas Escriche, Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger, Sara Wack). Without direct EB engagement, there is no validation of budget, commercial appetite, or executive sponsorship. The RFI process and the $1.58M indicative pricing submission suggest that budget exists and that procurement has been authorized at the exec level, but the absence of any commercial or pricing discussion with Kahlmeyer or another C-level sponsor is a red flag. Sara Wack also holds a Decision Maker role but her title (SCM PI IT S) suggests a functional/IT lead, not a budget holder. The gap: no evidence of who will approve the $1.58M spend or negotiate commercial terms.

sources: SF Next_Step__c RFI schedule 14 Apr 2026
SF Next_Step__c 30 Apr shortlist feedback
SF Next_Step__c 8 Jun demo
SF OpportunityContactRole: evaluators and Decision Makers
decision_process.narrative: Siemens is running a formal, multi-stage procurement process with a published timeline and governance. The process is: RFI document issued 27 Feb, provider confirmation 6 Mar, Q&A submission 13 Mar, answers 27 Mar, offer submission 10 Apr, shortlist feedback 30 Apr, demo sessions May-Jun, RFP Aug-Oct. Zycus passed the RFI shortlist gate (30 Apr) and delivered a demo (8 Jun) described as strong. The next gate is the RFP round (Aug-Oct), which will require completion of the five deliverables and likely a second round of demos or deeper use-case validation. The process is transparent and structured, controlled by procurement and the evaluator team (Viktor Kosov, Marta Pallas Escriche, Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger). The approval chain is not documented, but the involvement of a Senior VP (Sebastian Kahlmeyer) as Decision Maker and the $1.58M deal size suggest board or C-level sign-off will be required. The gap: who approves the final commercial terms, and whether there is a single final decision maker or a committee vote.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: five EPICs and five deliverables
SF Next_Step__c 8 May 2026: demo content confirmed with Siemens
SF Next_Step__c 8 Jun 2026: strong demo
SF Next_Step__c RFI schedule: Q&A phase, offer submission, shortlist feedback
decision_criteria.narrative: The RFI process structured around five EPICs and a detailed Q&A phase (13 Mar question submission, 27 Mar answers, 10 Apr offer submission) indicates that Siemens has documented business requirements and evaluation criteria. The demo on 8 Jun was described as 'strong' (Next_Step__c 8 Jun) and the content was confirmed with Siemens on 8 May, implying that Zycus aligned to the EPICs and met the evaluation bar for the demo phase. The RFI shortlist feedback on 30 Apr confirms that Zycus advanced past the initial filter. However, the specific criteria—what weight Siemens places on AI/Merlin capabilities, SAP integration depth, cybersecurity compliance, or commercial flexibility—are not documented. The five open deliverables (SCM STAR response, AI governance, SAP integration architecture, cybersecurity pack, EPIC alignment) suggest that these are the confirmed criteria for RFP qualification, but their relative importance and Siemens' scoring or weighting are unknown. The criteria are real but not transparent.

items.detail: 31-day buyer silence post-demo with no reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach. Siemens sent a polite thank-you on 10 Jun but proposed no forward step, and has not engaged since. This signals either (a) internal evaluation/pause before RFP, or (b) another vendor is advancing and Zycus is being back-burnered. No champion to provide internal visibility. Risk: Zycus is not the preferred vendor and the silence is a soft decline.

Economic Buyer (Sebastian Kahlmeyer, Senior VP & Data Officer) and Decision Maker (Sara Wack) have never been engaged. All demos and RFI interactions were with evaluators. Without EB validation of budget, commercial terms, and executive sponsorship, the deal cannot progress past the evaluator tier into commercial and contracting phases. Risk: evaluators like the solution but the EB/DM tier rejects it on commercial, strategic, or political grounds.

Five critical workstreams (SCM STAR response, AI governance documentation, SAP integration architecture validation, cybersecurity compliance pack, demo storyline alignment to 5 EPICs) have been open since 14 Apr with no completion recorded. If these are prerequisites for RFP submission (Aug to Oct), Zycus may miss the RFP window or submit an incomplete response. Risk: incomplete RFP response or disqualification.

No competitive intelligence. RFI shortlist implies 2-3 other vendors, but their identities, strengths, and Siemens' relative positioning of Zycus are unknown. Without this, Zycus cannot refine the differentiation wedge or the RFP response. Risk: blind to competitive threats and unable to position against them.

19-day buyer silence post-demo with no reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach. Siemens sent a polite thank-you on 10 Jun but proposed no forward step, and has not engaged since. This signals either (a) internal evaluation/pause before RFP, or (b) another vendor is advancing and Zycus is being back-burnered. No champion to provide internal visibility. Risk: Zycus is not the preferred vendor and the silence is a soft decline.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. All engagement with evaluators (Viktor Kosov, Marta Pallas Escriche, Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger). Positive demo feedback but no internal advocacy or access to EB/DM. 19-day silence and no reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach signal passive evaluator support, not active championship.

Do not chase the 22 Jun outreach generically; lead with value.

Amount stable at $1.58M. No forward slip, but 19-day buyer silence post-demo and five overdue deliverables signal stall risk.

RFP window Aug-Oct, but no champion to provide internal visibility and five deliverables open since 14 Apr. EB/DM never engaged. Evaluator-tier approval alone cannot advance to commercial phase.

flags: Competitive intelligence gap: RFI shortlist implies 2-3 other vendors, but their identities, strengths, and Siemens' relative positioning of Zycus are unknown. Without this, Zycus cannot refine the differentiation wedge or the RFP response. Immediate move: ask Viktor Kosov or another evaluator who else is on the shortlist and how Siemens is scoring the vendors.

Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged: Sebastian Kahlmeyer (Senior VP & Data Officer SCM Digitalization, Decision Maker role) has not participated in any call or meeting. He is the likely EB given his title and role, but budget authority, commercial approval, and executive sponsorship are unconfirmed. Immediate move: secure a meeting with Sebastian to validate the business case, discuss commercial terms, and secure executive sponsorship before the RFP phase.

Champion gap: no active champion driving the deal internally. Viktor Kosov (Functional Excellence lead) is the strongest evaluator candidate but has been transactional only. The 31-day silence and lack of reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach signal no one is pushing the deal forward on Siemens' side. Immediate move: develop Viktor into a coach by offering him competitive intelligence (how Zycus stacks up vs. the field) and a clear path to win, or secure a champion at the Decision Maker tier.

deltas.subject: 19-day buyer silence: last touch 10 Jun, Dirk reached out 22 Jun with no reply

31-day buyer silence post-demo with no reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach. Siemens sent a polite thank-you on 10 Jun but proposed no forward step, and has not engaged since. This signals either (a) internal evaluation/pause before RFP, or (b) another vendor is advancing and Zycus is being back-burnered. No champion to provide internal visibility. Risk: Zycus is not the preferred vendor and the silence is a soft decline.

Competitive intelligence gap: RFI shortlist implies 2-3 other vendors, but their identities, strengths, and Siemens' relative positioning of Zycus are unknown. Without this, Zycus cannot refine the differentiation wedge or the RFP response. Immediate move: ask Viktor Kosov or another evaluator who else is on the shortlist and how Siemens is scoring the vendors.

Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged: Sebastian Kahlmeyer (Senior VP & Data Officer SCM Digitalization, Decision Maker role) has not participated in any call or meeting. He is the likely EB given his title and role, but budget authority, commercial approval, and executive sponsorship are unconfirmed. Immediate move: secure a meeting with Sebastian to validate the business case, discuss commercial terms, and secure executive sponsorship before the RFP phase.

Champion gap: no active champion driving the deal internally. Viktor Kosov (Functional Excellence lead) is the strongest evaluator candidate but has been transactional only. The 31-day silence and lack of reply to Dirk's 22 Jun outreach signal no one is pushing the deal forward on Siemens' side. Immediate move: develop Viktor into a coach by offering him competitive intelligence (how Zycus stacks up vs. the field) and a clear path to win, or secure a champion at the Decision Maker tier.
